newsSince: No confirmed company-specific announcement explains the +31% move since June 9. Formosa Sumco (Taiwan JV affiliated with Japan's SUMCO) is riding the same industry-wide 300mm/silicon-wafer tightness and price-hike narrative as its parent (Nomura demand upgrade, projected >20% shortage through 2030). The spike appears momentum/flow-led rather than driven by a disclosed catalyst.

sources.label: Semiecosystem (Lapedus) - Silicon wafer upturn, higher prices
TrendForce - SUMCO delays two wafer fabs amid market shift

verdicts.rankRationale: Test-time-scaling / silicon-consumption basket: the industry wafer-tightness thesis genuinely strengthened (300mm price hikes, >20% shortage to 2030), modestly improving the silicon-supply case. But the +31% spike is momentum with no disclosed company catalyst; I do not reward the price move. Hold, +1.
verdicts.bull: AI capacity needs more wafers; tightening supply lifts pricing for all 12-inch suppliers.
verdicts.neutral: Small specialist supplier riding parent SUMCO's narrative; no own catalyst.
verdicts.bear: +285% YTD; +31% spike is flow; China wafer price offensive; thin direct AI link.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: 300mm wafer price hikes flowing to Taiwan suppliers
